Business Analysis Capability
I lead digital initiatives end to end — from requirements to a live, measured result
Stakeholder engagement, requirements gathering, workshop facilitation, user story writing and backlog refinement — applied across every enterprise project in this portfolio.
🤝
Stakeholder Engagement
Run structured discovery sessions with Legal, UX, Marketing, Technology, and Content leads. Translate competing priorities into a single agreed direction. Reported to CITO and SteerCo at AFAC with weekly outcome updates on a 90-day plan.
SteerCo reportingCITO-sponsoredCross-functionalExecutive comms
📋
Requirements Gathering
Open Confluence initiative pages before any design work: background, objective, RACI, RAID log. Interview stakeholders to surface the real problem — not the assumed one. At ASB, translated simultaneous, conflicting requirements from four teams into one practical AEM architecture decision.
ConfluenceRAID logsRACIGap analysis
🗂️
Workshop Facilitation
Facilitate kickoff workshops with Marketing, Content, UX, Product, Legal and a lead developer. Use Confluence Whiteboard for live Post-it note user-flow mapping. Run progressive workshops — discovery first, then design alignment, then story mapping — so decisions build on each other rather than being revisited.
Kickoff workshopsUser-flow mappingWhiteboard facilitationDesign alignment

User Stories & Backlog

Prioritise trade-offs as real options (see NAFC Fleet A/B/C) so stakeholders decide with full context.

Building With AI
I don't just document CMS and content problems — I'm now building tools to solve them
Applying AI directly to the delivery and governance challenges described throughout this portfolio — not as side experiments, but as tools I actively use.
Self-Initiated · Live Tool · No Login Required
AI Accessibility Checker
Grounded in the same WCAG 2.1 AA, Lighthouse, and legal sign-off standards used to ship every page on the AFAC migration — not generic accessibility advice.
Live
In active use on the AFAC 4-site migration
0
Login required to try it
Mins
Compiling time, down from hours
Problem
Every page has to clear the same gate

Accessibility, performance, SEO and legal sign-off — checked manually, page by page, before anything goes live.

Approach
Automate the first pass, keep the judgment

Built an AI tool grounded in the real standards I sign off against, so findings are accurate and immediately actionable — not generic advice.

✓ What It Delivers
01
Scored dashboard
Every issue prioritised by effort — quick win, needs dev, or needs formal sign-off.
02
Jira & Confluence-ready output
Acceptance criteria and a sign-off table, formatted for the workflow already in use — paste and go.
03
Business-impact summary on every issue
Findings are decision-ready for stakeholders, with no separate translation step required.
Outcome
Where it's earning its keep

Used live on the AFAC WordPress → Directus migration to work through content and accessibility issues as they surface — making it easier to bring stakeholders clear, evidence-backed recommendations, with the impact already framed.

Try the Checker →
Self-Initiated · Live Product · Guided AI
Drona AI — ARC
Structured workflows for Business Analysts and Product Owners — built around real BA/PO thinking, not a blank chatbot.
3
Live workflows: User Stories, Backlog Refinement, Sprint Planning
0
Prompts to write — inputs are guided
Problem
Blank chatboxes don't match how BAs and POs actually work

Turning messy delivery input — a Slack thread, a vague ask, a half-formed backlog item — into something review-ready still depends on knowing what to ask an AI, and how to structure the result.

Approach
Guided workflows, not open-ended prompting

Each workflow is designed around BA/PO delivery thinking — readiness, assumptions, risks, scope and effort — so the person coordinating the work doesn't have to invent a prompt to get a consistent, structured output.

✓ Live Workflows
01
User Story Studio
Generates stories, acceptance criteria, assumptions, and clarifying questions from raw input.
02
Backlog Refinement
INVEST checks, readiness review, risk flags, and story-splitting recommendations.
03
Sprint Planning
Capacity, scope, effort, dependencies, and planning risks — laid out for stakeholder review.
Outcome
From messy input to coordinated action

Every workflow takes unclear delivery input and turns it into a predictable, review-friendly output — capture, structure, generate — so BAs and POs spend less time formatting and more time deciding.

Case Studies
The work, with evidence
Select a tab to explore the full Problem → Approach → Deliverables → Outcome → Business Impact for each project.
AFAC · May 2026 – Present · Directus 11.4.1 · CITO-sponsored
NAFC Rebuilt: end-to-end CMS migration with architecture, relational data model, editorial workflows and org-wide documentation
Scope: Homepage · Fleet landing page · 6 aircraft detail pages · 90-day plan · weekly SteerCo reporting
–
developer deps
at launch
0
Bond component
types orchestrated
0
relational
collections modelled
0
playbooks
adopted org-wide
"

When I joined AFAC, the NAFC microsite was a WordPress legacy with no content model, no relational data, and editors filing helpdesk tickets every time they needed to update an aircraft record. I proposed rebuilding it entirely inside Directus using only existing Bond components — no custom frontend code, no developer budget. Fourteen weeks later it was live, the architecture was adopted as the org reference model, and I had two playbooks in production use before the contract review.

Vaibhav Bhor · Senior Website Experience Lead · AFAC
How I approached this
🔍
Discovery & Stakeholder Interviews
Ran structured discovery with Ellie (content lead) and Darren (program owner) before any wireframe or architecture work. Surfaced editor pain points and SteerCo expectations in week one.
📋
RAID Log & 90-Day Delivery Plan
Maintained a live RAID log throughout the program. Weekly outcome reports to SteerCo structured against the 90-day plan — each week's delivery tracked against scope, risk and dependency.
🗂️
Requirements Specification
Translated stakeholder inputs into a structured Directus architecture spec: data model requirements, permission matrix, editorial workflow rules and component mapping — all documented before build.
⚖️
Options Analysis & Trade-off Presentation
Presented three fleet-page options (A/B/C) with build cost, content-model complexity and editor workload mapped against each. Stakeholder decision documented before a single collection was created.
✅
Acceptance Criteria & UAT
Defined acceptance criteria per deliverable: each page signed off against WCAG 2.1 AA, Lighthouse score threshold, mobile rendering and editorial workflow test. Zero launch-day defects.
⚠ Problem
Legacy WordPress CMS, no data model, no architecture

NAFC public microsite had no content model, no relational data structure and no editor governance. Aircraft data sat in a flat table. Navigation duplicated AFAC's parent nav. No primary task for a first-time visitor. Constraint: rebuild inside Directus and the existing Bond component library with zero developer budget.

→ Approach
Architecture-first — stakeholder workshops before any authoring

Ran discovery with Ellie and Darren. Set Lighthouse and WAVE baselines before redesign began. Designed the 6-layer Directus architecture (Schema → Permissions → Files → Flows → Pages → Launch) before touching a single page. Delivered weekly outcome reports to SteerCo on a 90-day plan.

✓ Key Deliverables
01
6-layer Directus architecture
Schema → Permissions → Files → Flows → Pages → Launch. Now the reference model for all future AFAC microsite migrations.
02
Fleet domain data model
5 relational collections, UUID primary keys, M2O/O2M/M2M relationships. Replaced a flat 175-row operator table with a queryable, editor-friendly model.
03
CMS-only UX rebuild
Zero custom frontend code, zero developer dependency at launch. 6 Bond component types orchestrated entirely through Directus authoring.
04
Editorial workflow automation
Directus Flows: Submit for Review → Approval Notification → Auto-Publish. Editor-controlled publishing without extension code.
05
Documentation adopted org-wide
Implementation Workbook + Build Playbook v2.0 adopted as onboarding and migration references across AFAC.
↑ Outcome
Live on afac.org.au · zero dev dependency
  • Homepage, fleet landing and 6 aircraft detail pages live
  • Architecture became org reference model for 4-site program
  • Both playbooks adopted before contract end
★ Business Impact
Reusable platform for 3 remaining site migrations

Future sites start from a working architecture, data model and editorial workflow — reducing risk and delivery time across the entire AFAC program.

📄 Executive Case Study — NAFC Rebuilt (one-pager)
⟳ Visual Evidence — Homepage before & after
AFAC · NAFC Fleet Page · BA Decision Artifact + UX Rebuild
From a 175-row unstyled data table to a browsable fleet — with three stakeholder options presented before any build decision
Drag the slider to compare the before and after live on afac.org.au
"

The brief said 'improve the fleet page.' When I opened it, I found a raw 175-row data table with no imagery, no filters, and no way for a visitor to understand what a Type 2 helicopter actually does. Before I designed anything, I mapped three structurally different options — a card overview, a static table, and a filterable live table — each with its build cost and ongoing editor effort laid out honestly. The stakeholder chose. Then I built.

Vaibhav Bhor · AFAC NAFC Fleet Page
How I approached this
📊
Current-State Analysis
Audited the existing 175-row flat table: identified absence of filtering, grouping, imagery and detail pages as the core UX failures before proposing any solution.
⚖️
Options Analysis — A/B/C Decision Artifact
Mapped three structurally distinct options with build effort, Directus content-model complexity and ongoing editor workload for each. Stakeholder chose Option A — card-based overview with download. Documented and actioned.
🗃️
Data Modelling
Designed the 5-collection relational fleet data model (aircraft, operators, bases, capability tiers, images) with UUID primary keys and M2O/O2M/M2M relationships — replacing the flat table with a queryable, editor-friendly structure.
👥
Stakeholder Sign-off
Each design decision gate (options selection, data model approval, component mapping) formally signed off by Darren before proceeding. No build without confirmed direction.
⚠ Problem
175 aircraft, one unstyled table
  • No filtering or grouping by aircraft type or capability
  • No aircraft imagery or individual detail pages
  • Data maintained manually in a flat operator table
  • Visitors couldn't understand fleet capability at a glance
→ Approach
Three options presented before any build decision

Mapped three real options (A/B/C) with build effort, Directus content-model complexity and ongoing editor workload trade-offs. Darren and the team made an informed call — they didn't inherit a default.

⇔
← Before: raw 175-row table, no imagery, no filters
After: card-based, grouped, live on afac.org.au →
📐 BA Artifact — stakeholder options presented
↑ Outcome
Card-based fleet, live
  • Grouped by Rotary Wing / Fixed Wing and capability tier
  • 150+ aircraft surfaced with a clear hero stat
  • Individual aircraft detail pages built as Directus items
  • Zero custom frontend code
★ Business Impact
Fleet data is now editor-managed and queryable

The 5-collection relational data model means AFAC editors can add, update and retire aircraft without developer involvement — supporting the full 4-site program.

AFAC · NAFC Tenders & Procurement
Turning a buried procurement process into a four-step user journey — mapped, validated, then built
Process mapping · Stakeholder validation · Information architecture · CMS authoring
How I approached this
🔍
Process Discovery & Mapping
Ran a structured discovery session to map the ARENA procurement process end to end: Tender Release → Evaluation → Contract Award. Documented supplier pain points — specifically the absence of a visible process on the page.
🎨
Three Concept Design Options
Presented Concept 1 (Modern & Bold), Concept 2 (Clean & Minimal) and Concept 3 (Visual & Engaging) to stakeholder with information hierarchy rationale for each. One concept selected before build commenced.
✍️
User Story Definition
Wrote user stories for each page section from the supplier's perspective: "As a supplier, I need to see the current open tender without navigating away" — each story mapped to a specific Directus block.
✅
Validation Before Build
Page structure validated by Darren and the procurement team before authoring. Zero direction changes after build commenced — the front-loaded decision sessions absorbed all ambiguity.
⚠ Problem
Process buried in PDFs — suppliers had no pathway
  • ARENA procurement process (find → register → prepare → submit) not visible on-page
  • Current opportunities, process guidance and contact all in separate locations
  • Suppliers calling NAFC for information that should be self-serve
→ Approach
Requirements gathered, process mapped, then validated

Ran discovery with Darren to map the end-to-end ARENA procurement process. Designed page structure that surfaces current opportunities, 4-step process, procurement rules and help on a single page. Validated with stakeholder before any authoring began.

📐 BA Artifact — three design concepts presented to stakeholder before build
✓ Delivered — visual evidence
↑ Outcome
One page, full supplier journey
  • Current open opportunity in a prominent card
  • 4-step ARENA process rendered visually — not in a PDF
  • Procurement information, submission guide and contact all on-page
  • Subscribe for Alerts CTA in hero and footer
★ Business Impact
Supplier self-service — fewer inbound enquiries

Suppliers now have a clear start-to-finish pathway without contacting NAFC. The visual process walkthrough and on-page help reduce support load during active tender windows.

AFAC · National Emergency Services Memorial
Wireframe → Directus architecture → live Memorial site — with a build guide so editors can run it independently
Low-fi wireframing · User journey mapping · Directus CMS build · Editor enablement documentation
"

This one required care before it required code. The Memorial honours over 700 fire and emergency service personnel who died in the line of duty — families use it to search for loved ones. I spent the first week mapping every user path with a low-fidelity wireframe before a single visual was designed, because getting the structure wrong here wasn't a UX problem, it was a human one. The wireframe became the stakeholder alignment document. The Directus build came after.

Vaibhav Bhor · AFAC National Emergency Services Memorial
How I approached this
🗺️
User Journey Mapping
Built a full low-fidelity user-flow wireframe covering every key path: Homepage → Honour Roll search → Individual profile → Gallery → Events → Contact. Used as the primary stakeholder alignment artifact before any visual design.
👥
Sensitive Stakeholder Engagement
The Memorial serves families of deceased emergency service personnel. Discovery sessions with AFAC's Memorial team shaped the information hierarchy around family needs (search by name, year, service) not organisational structure.
📋
Functional Requirements
Defined functional requirements for the Honour Roll: searchable by name, year, service and location; filterable to 38 pages of results; individual profile pages with biography, photo and related profiles. Translated directly into Directus data model.
📖
Editor Enablement Documentation
Authored a click-by-click Directus build guide for non-technical AFAC editors. Adopted as the team's onboarding reference — the documentation outlasted the contract.
⚠ Problem
No IA existed for a sensitive, high-traffic memorial

The site needed to honour 700+ names on the Memorial Wall, surface upcoming events and let families find individual profiles. No information architecture existed. Required editor-managed delivery and an emotionally appropriate structure.

→ Approach
Map the user journey before any design or build work

Built a low-fidelity wireframe and user-flow diagram covering all paths: homepage → honour roll search → individual profile → gallery → events → contact. Used this as the stakeholder alignment artifact. Then designed the Directus data model and component architecture to match the mapped flows.

✓ Wireframe → build guide → live site
↑ Outcome
Live site, fully editor-managed
  • Searchable Honour Roll: 700+ profiles with name, year, service, panel, biography
  • Upcoming events with Add to Calendar
  • Gallery with category filtering
  • Click-by-click build guide adopted as AFAC onboarding reference
★ Business Impact
Families find loved ones without contacting AFAC

Searchable Honour Roll with advanced filtering lets families find inscribed names independently. Editors add profiles, events and gallery content without developer involvement.

ASB Bank (CBA Group) · Aug 2023 – Jun 2024 · Auckland · AEM Classic → Touch
Leading a 300-page AEM migration inside full CBA enterprise governance — CF model design, multi-team requirements synthesis, zero rework
Requirements gathering · Workshop facilitation · AEM CF/XF modelling · Migration governance · Training
0
SME banking
pages migrated
–
post-launch
rework
100%
WCAG 2.1 AA
& legal sign-off
0
stakeholder
teams aligned
"

I arrived at ASB to find 300+ pages of SME banking content with no migration path and no content model for AEM Touch. Legal had their requirements. UX had theirs. Marketing had theirs. Technology had theirs. None of them agreed. My first three weeks weren't spent in the CMS — they were spent in a whiteboard room, translating four sets of conflicting requirements into one architecture decision everyone could live with. That's what made the zero-rework result possible.

Vaibhav Bhor · ASB Bank (CBA Group) · AEM Migration Lead
How I approached this
🔍
Multi-Stakeholder Requirements Gathering
Facilitated structured workshops across Legal, UX, Marketing and Technology — four teams with simultaneous, often conflicting requirements. Documented each team's non-negotiables, then synthesised into a single agreed AEM Content Fragment architecture.
🗂️
Content Fragment Data Modelling
Designed CF models from scratch: field types, validation rules, content relationships and author constraints defined before migration began. The BA equivalent of data modelling — removing ambiguity at source rather than resolving it post-launch.
⚠️
Risk & Dependency Management
Maintained a migration RAID log covering AEM Classic deprecation timeline, Legal sign-off gates, UX design freeze dates and offshore team availability. Each dependency tracked to resolution before the corresponding pages migrated.
📖
Governance Documentation & Handover
Authored migration playbooks and SOPs adopted by the ASB Web Solutions team as their standard. Junior producer training documented and delivered. Sustainable BAU capability left on exit — work that outlasted the contract.
✅
Compliance Acceptance Criteria
Every migrated page signed off against WCAG 2.1 AA, CBA brand standards and Legal review before publication. 100% sign-off rate across 300+ pages. Zero post-launch rework.
⚠ Problem
No CF model, four teams with conflicting requirements

ASB needed to retire AEM Classic for 300+ SME banking pages inside full CBA enterprise risk and compliance frameworks. No Content Fragment model existed. Legal, UX, Marketing and Technology all had simultaneous, often conflicting requirements with no single point of resolution.

→ Approach (BA-first)
Workshops first, content model second, migration third

Facilitated multi-team workshops to surface real constraints before any authoring. Designed AEM CF models from scratch — field types, validation rules, relationships — resolving conflicts upfront. Built governance documentation and playbooks. Trained junior producers on AEM Touch authoring for sustainable BAU handover.

✓ Key Deliverables
01
Multi-team requirements workshop facilitation
Legal, UX, Marketing and Technology requirements synthesised in structured workshops — producing one agreed AEM architecture decision with no ambiguity downstream.
02
AEM Content Fragment models designed from scratch
Field types, validation rules and content relationships defined before migration — the BA equivalent of data modelling for a CMS environment.
03
Migration governance documentation
Playbooks adopted by the ASB Web Solutions team as their standard — work that outlasted the contract.
04
Junior producer training and BAU handover
Trained and mentored junior content producers on AEM Touch authoring. Sustainable handover on contract exit.
↑ Outcome
300+ pages migrated, zero post-launch rework

Upfront CF model design eliminated authoring ambiguity so completely that no rework was required after launch. 100% WCAG 2.1 AA, brand and legal compliance across all pages.

★ Business Impact
CBA-approved migration model and permanent team uplift

Migration governance playbooks became ASB Web Solutions' standard. Trained junior team operates AEM Touch independently. Zero rework cost saved significant post-launch effort and risk.

🖥 Portfolio slide — ASB Bank AEM Classic to Touch Migration
🖥 Visual — ASB Business Hub (asb.co.nz/businesshub)
ANZ Bank · Jul 2024 – Jul 2025 · Melbourne · AEM · Deposits Business Unit
GA4-led diagnosis, IA restructure and a 43.6% uplift — across a multi-page Brokerology hub with Build Your Business, Articles & Insights and Become Accredited
GA4 analysis · Information architecture · Agile squad BA support · AEM XF authoring · QA governance
43.6%
uplift in unique
visitors — GA4 verified
5,845
unique visitors
post-restructure
0
distinct hub
content types built
–
rework requests
post-launch
"

The brief was vague: the Brokerology hub wasn't performing. My instinct was to open GA4 before opening AEM. What I found wasn't a design problem — it was an information architecture problem. Brokers were landing on the right section but losing the thread at the second level of navigation because the content groupings didn't match how they thought about the product. I restructured the IA in AEM with zero frontend dev time, set a baseline before launch, and measured 43.6% more unique visitors after. Along the way I also hit a compliance issue in AEM — duplicate disclaimer reference numbers on the Mobile Lenders page — and resolved it with a content fragment child page workaround that required no developer involvement. Sometimes the best solutions are the simplest.

Vaibhav Bhor · ANZ Bank · Deposits Business Unit
How I approached this
📊
GA4 Diagnostic Analysis
Treated the brief as a BA discovery exercise: opened GA4 before opening AEM. Identified the specific second-level navigation groupings causing broker drop-off — evidence-led diagnosis, not assumption-led redesign.
🗺️
Information Architecture Restructure
Mapped the existing Brokerology content structure against broker mental models. Redesigned groupings and labels in AEM authoring only — no frontend dev time. Set pre-change baseline, measured post-change uplift: 43.6% more unique visitors.
🤝
Agile Squad BA Support
Supported the Deposits Business Unit squad with backlog refinement, feasibility assessment and build-complexity guidance across sprints. Reduced ambiguity between business, design and engineering at each sprint boundary.
📋
Multi-Stakeholder Content Governance
Legal, Brand and UX reviewed the same content simultaneously using version-controlled copy decks tracked October 2024 to January 2025. Governance embedded upstream — not checked at the publish gate.
📖
Training & Capability Transfer
Delivered AEM authoring workshops for the ANZ Media Team. Training materials now adopted as ANZ's standard publishing guidelines — permanent capability uplift embedded in the team.
🎨
Design Collaboration — Figma & FigJam
Worked with design teams in Figma and FigJam contributing to layout decisions and visual consistency. Ensured image assets were correctly sized and formatted for the Article List Component — auto-pull for article pages, manual assignment for non-article pages.
📂
Project Coordination & Documentation
Maintained clear Jira documentation of blockers, approvals and sandpit page references throughout. Managed preview and production links, facilitating staged stakeholder reviews across Legal, Brand and UX simultaneously.
⚠ Problem
Brokerology hub underperforming — root cause unknown

The ANZ Brokerology content hub was underperforming. Rather than accepting a vague brief to "redesign it," I opened GA4 first — treating this as a BA discovery exercise before any design or authoring work began.

→ Approach
Diagnose first, restructure second, measure third

Used GA4 to identify exactly where users lost the thread. Restructured information architecture — grouping, labelling, hierarchy — without a visual redesign. Set GA4 baseline pre-change. Remeasured post-launch. 43.6% uplift in unique visitors — measured, not assumed.

Collaborated with design teams in Figma and FigJam on layout decisions and visual consistency. Managed preview and production links throughout, facilitating staged stakeholder reviews. Maintained Jira documentation of blockers, approvals and sandpit page references across the full delivery lifecycle.

✓ Key Deliverables
01
GA4 content friction analysis
Identified specific pages and sections causing drop-off. Evidence-led brief — not an assumption-led redesign request. GA4 confirmed: 8,596 total visits, 5,845 unique visitors, 43.58% period-on-period uplift.
02
Multi-page hub architecture — three content types
Built Your Business, Articles & Insights and Become Accredited each had distinct component logic. Content Fragments powered the Article List Component with auto-pull image handling for article pages and manual image assignment for non-article pages — a structural distinction I defined and documented.
03
Information architecture restructure
Regrouped and relabelled Brokerology hub content via AEM authoring only — no frontend development required.
04
Agile squad BA support
Backlog refinement, feasibility assessment and build-complexity guidance across Deposits Business Unit sprints — reducing ambiguity between business, design and engineering.
05
QA standards and ANZ Media Team training
Built pre-publish QA standards covering WCAG 2.1 AA, metadata, analytics tagging and responsive rendering. Delivered AEM authoring workshops now adopted as ANZ's standard publishing guidelines.
🖥 Portfolio slide — ANZ Bank Brokerology Hub · 43.6% uplift
🖥 Visual — ANZ Brokerology Hub (anz.com.au/promo/broker)
🔧 Bonus: Constraint-driven technical problem solving
Resolving disclaimer duplication in AEM — ANZ Mobile Lenders
⚠ THE PROBLEM
AEM treated each disclaimer instance as unique — displaying the same legal text with different reference numbers. Flagged as a legal compliance risk by stakeholders.
💡 THE SOLUTION
Created a Content Fragment reference using the original disclaimer path as a child page. Both instances pointed to the same source — identical content, same reference number, no duplication.
✓ OUTCOME
Consistent disclaimer display across the page. Legal and UX integrity maintained. Stakeholder trust strengthened through proactive communication of the fix.
Sometimes the best solutions are the simplest — a clean workaround that required no developer involvement and no platform changes.
↑ Outcome
43.6% uplift — GA4 confirmed

The fix cost zero frontend dev time. QA standards and workshop materials embedded permanently in the ANZ Media Team — outlasting the contract. Delivered a scalable, compliant and engaging broker hub reflecting ANZ's strategic priorities: Build Your Business, Articles & Insights and Become Accredited, each with distinct component architecture and a documented content model.

★ Business Impact
More brokers reached · team capability permanently uplifted

43.6% more unique visitors means meaningfully more broker touchpoints with ANZ Deposits content. Published QA standards gave the ANZ Media Team a permanent operating baseline.

2degrees · Oct 2019 – Aug 2023 · Auckland · Drupal + Silverstripe CMS
iPhone 11 launch page built entirely in-house — $20,000+ in agency costs saved, Apple liaison handled directly
CMS delivery · Direct Apple stakeholder liaison · Brand compliance · UX standards maintained to Apple spec
$20K+
agency fees
saved
0
supplier campaigns
delivered
0
years CMS
production
How I approached this
💡
Constraint-Driven Problem Solving
Identified that the $20,000+ agency cost was unnecessary given Silverstripe CMS capability and Apple's available brand kit. Proposed the in-house build as a formal internal business case, managing risk including direct Apple NZ liaison.
🤝
Direct Supplier Stakeholder Management
Managed Apple NZ brand approval directly — the relationship normally routed through the agency intermediary. Submitted the page for Apple sign-off and received approval without revision on a hard launch-day deadline.
📋
Campaign Requirements Coordination
Coordinated Marketing, Product and Legal sign-off simultaneously across every supplier campaign. Maintained brand and accessibility standards under Apple-announcement-day time pressure across 40+ campaigns over four years.
🔄
Reusable Component Framework
Built reusable landing page templates and component patterns across Apple, Samsung, Huawei and Oppo ranges. Each new supplier launch reused the framework — reducing build time and review cycles on every subsequent campaign.
🎨
Visual & UX Collaboration
Worked closely with designers to align visual assets with campaign goals. Collaborated with developers to troubleshoot responsive design issues and ensure layouts rendered correctly across mobile and desktop devices under launch-day time pressure.
♿
SEO & Accessibility Compliance
Embedded SEO and accessibility compliance into every campaign page — metadata optimisation, alt text, heading hierarchy and responsive rendering checked before publication. Apple brand guidelines required Apple-standard accessibility on every page.
⚠ Problem
Agency quoted $20K+ to build the iPhone 11 launch page

2degrees' digital team needed a high-fidelity iPhone 11 launch experience — matching Apple's exacting brand and UX standards — for the NZ market. The standard route was to commission an external agency, at a cost of $20,000+. The timeline was tight, the budget was under pressure, and the brief had to meet Apple's brand guidelines exactly or it wouldn't be approved.

→ Approach
Liaised directly with Apple NZ — cut the agency out entirely

Proposed building the page in-house using 2degrees' Silverstripe CMS, matching Apple's design kit to pixel-level precision. Liaised directly with Apple's NZ marketing team to get assets, brand guidelines and sign-off — the relationship normally managed through the agency. Coordinated QA, accessibility checks and legal review internally before submitting for Apple approval. Worked closely with design teams to align visual assets with campaign goals, ensuring SEO and accessibility compliance across all pages.

🖥 Portfolio slide — 2degrees Apple product range landing pages
🖥 Visual — 2degrees iPhone 11 launch page (2degrees.nz)

End-to-end CMS execution, stakeholder coordination and QA on every campaign — Apple, Samsung, Huawei and Oppo — across Drupal and Silverstripe.
"

The agency quote came in at over $20,000 to build the iPhone 11 launch page. I looked at the brief, looked at our Silverstripe CMS, looked at Apple's brand kit — and proposed building it in-house. The catch: I'd need to manage the Apple NZ relationship directly, which normally went through the agency. I did. Apple signed it off without a revision. The page launched on time, matched Apple's standard, and $20,000 stayed in the budget. After that, we never outsourced a supplier launch page again.

Vaibhav Bhor · 2degrees · iPhone 11 Launch
✓ Key Deliverables
01
iPhone 11 launch page — built in-house, Apple-approved
Matched Apple's brand guidelines and UX standards pixel-perfectly within Silverstripe CMS. Submitted directly to Apple NZ for sign-off and approved without revision.
02
Direct Apple NZ stakeholder liaison
Managed the Apple relationship that would normally require an agency intermediary — asset acquisition, brand guideline review, approval sign-off, all handled in-house.
03
40+ supplier campaigns delivered over 4 years
Apple, Samsung, Huawei and Oppo campaigns delivered with end-to-end CMS execution, stakeholder coordination and QA across Drupal and Silverstripe.
04
CMS migration SME during Silverstripe → Drupal platform move
Served as Silverstripe CMS custodian throughout the platform migration — mapping content models, metadata and component behaviour to ensure zero content loss.
↑ Outcome
$20,000+ saved · Apple-approved · launched on time
  • Launch page live on 2degrees.nz on iPhone 11 release day in NZ
  • Apple NZ brand guidelines met and approved — no agency required
  • $20,000+ in external agency costs avoided
  • Precedent set for future in-house Apple campaign delivery
★ Business Impact
In-house capability proved — agency dependency reduced across the whole supplier campaign program

The iPhone 11 project established that 2degrees' internal digital team could meet Apple's exacting brand standards without agency intermediaries. This reduced external spend and gave the team direct supplier relationships — most visibly with Apple NZ — that benefited all subsequent campaigns in the program. Successfully launched flagship campaigns that drove traffic and conversions across device ecosystems. Reduced reliance on external agencies and strengthened brand presence through consistent, compliant and engaging digital experiences across Apple, Samsung, Oppo and Huawei.
